blocks.text: The Central Dogma of molecular biology explains the flow of genetic information, from DNA to RNA, to make a functional product, a protein.

lines.text: The Central Dogma of molecular biology explains the flow of genetic
information, from DNA to RNA, to make a functional product, a protein.

blocks.text: • Transcription: Synthesizing RNA from a DNA template (in nucleus).

lines.text: • Transcription: Synthesizing RNA from a DNA template (in nucleus).

blocks.text: • Translation: Synthesizing an amino acid sequence from an mRNA template (in ribosomes).

lines.text: • Translation: Synthesizing an amino acid sequence from an mRNA template

blocks.text: [Cite: "Molecular Biology of the Cell, Alberts et al."]

lines.text: [Cite: "Molecular Biology of the Cell, Alberts et al."]

blocks.text: Law of Segregation: Allele pairs separate during gamete formation, and randomly unite at fertilization.
Law of Independent Assortment: Alleles of two (or more) different genes get sorted into gametes independently of one another.
